item.title: PlayCoder: Making LLM-Generated GUI Code Playable
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved strong results in code generation, but their ability to generate GUI applications, especially games, remains insufficiently studied. Existing benchmarks mainly evaluate correctness through test...

item.title: MM-JudgeBias: A Benchmark for Evaluating Compositional Biases in MLLM-as-a-Judge
item.description: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have been increasingly used as automatic evaluators-a paradigm known as MLLM-as-a-Judge. However, their reliability and vulnerabilities to biases remain underexplored. We find that many MLLM judge...

item.title: Chat2Workflow: A Benchmark for Generating Executable Visual Workflows with Natural Language
item.description: At present, executable visual workflows have emerged as a mainstream paradigm in real-world industrial deployments, offering strong reliability and controllability. However, in current practice, such workflows are almost entirely constru...

item.title: SkillLearnBench: Benchmarking Continual Learning Methods for Agent Skill Generation on Real-World Tasks
item.description: Skills have become the de facto way to enable LLM agents to perform complex real-world tasks with customized instructions, workflows, and tools, but how to learn them automatically and effectively remains unclear. We introduce SkillLearn...

item.title: Mind's Eye: A Benchmark of Visual Abstraction, Transformation and Composition for Multimodal LLMs
item.description: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have achieved impressive progress on vision language benchmarks, yet their capacity for visual cognitive and visuospatial reasoning remains less understood. We introduce "Mind's Eye", a multiple-c...

item.title: AgentSPEX: An Agent SPecification and EXecution Language
item.description: Language-model agent systems commonly rely on reactive prompting, in which a single instruction guides the model through an open-ended sequence of reasoning and tool-use steps, leaving control flow and intermediate state implicit and mak...

item.title: ShadowPEFT: Shadow Network for Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning
item.description: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) reduces the training cost of full-parameter fine-tuning for large language models (LLMs) by training only a small set of task-specific parameters while freezing the pretrained backbone. However, exi...

item.title: AJ-Bench: Benchmarking Agent-as-a-Judge for Environment-Aware Evaluation
item.description: As reinforcement learning continues to scale the training of large language model-based agents, reliably verifying agent behaviors in complex environments has become increasingly challenging. Existing approaches rely on rule-based verifi...

item.title: Target-Oriented Pretraining Data Selection via Neuron-Activated Graph
item.description: Everyday tasks come with a target, and pretraining models around this target is what turns them into experts. In this paper, we study target-oriented language model (LM) pretraining by introducing Neuron-Activated Graph Ranking (NAG-base...

item.title: UDM-GRPO: Stable and Efficient Group Relative Policy Optimization for Uniform Discrete Diffusion Models
item.description: Uniform Discrete Diffusion Model (UDM) has recently emerged as a promising paradigm for discrete generative modeling; however, its integration with reinforcement learning remains largely unexplored. We observe that naively applying GRPO...

item.title: HP-Edit: A Human-Preference Post-Training Framework for Image Editing
item.description: Common image editing tasks typically adopt powerful generative diffusion models as the leading paradigm for real-world content editing. Meanwhile, although reinforcement learning (RL) methods such as Diffusion-DPO and Flow-GRPO have furt...

item.title: AnyRecon: Arbitrary-View 3D Reconstruction with Video Diffusion Model
item.description: Sparse-view 3D reconstruction is essential for modeling scenes from casual captures, but remain challenging for non-generative reconstruction. Existing diffusion-based approaches mitigates this issues by synthesizing novel views, but the...

item.title: Speculative Decoding for Autoregressive Video Generation
item.description: Autoregressive video diffusion is emerging as a promising paradigm for streaming video synthesis, with step distillation serving as the primary means of accelerating inference. Whether speculative decoding, the dominant acceleration stra...

item.title: What Makes an LLM a Good Optimizer? A Trajectory Analysis of LLM-Guided Evolutionary Search
item.description: Recent work has demonstrated the promise of orchestrating large language models (LLMs) within evolutionary and agentic optimization systems. However, the mechanisms driving these optimization gains remain poorly understood. In this work,...

item.title: Evaluation-driven Scaling for Scientific Discovery
item.description: Language models are increasingly used in scientific discovery to generate hypotheses, propose candidate solutions, implement systems, and iteratively refine them. At the core of these trial-and-error loops lies evaluation: the process of...

item.title: Contrastive Attribution in the Wild: An Interpretability Analysis of LLM Failures on Realistic Benchmarks
item.description: Interpretability tools are increasingly used to analyze failures of Large Language Models (LLMs), yet prior work largely focuses on short prompts or toy settings, leaving their behavior on commonly used benchmarks underexplored. To addre...

item.title: Mitigating Multimodal Hallucination via Phase-wise Self-reward
item.description: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) still struggle with vision hallucination, where generated responses are inconsistent with the visual input. Existing methods either rely on large-scale annotated data for fine-tuning, which incurs mas...

item.title: Chain-of-Thought Degrades Visual Spatial Reasoning Capabilities of Multimodal LLMs
item.description: Multimodal Reasoning Models (MRMs) leveraging Chain-of-Thought (CoT) based thinking have revolutionized mathematical and logical problem-solving. However, we show that this paradigm struggles with generalized spatial intelligence. We per...

item.title: The Cognitive Penalty: Ablating System 1 and System 2 Reasoning in Edge-Native SLMs for Decentralized Consensus
item.description: Decentralized Autonomous Organizations (DAOs) are inclined explore Small Language Models (SLMs) as edge-native constitutional firewalls to vet proposals and mitigate semantic social engineering. While scaling inference-time compute (Syst...

item.title: Tstars-Tryon 1.0: Robust and Realistic Virtual Try-On for Diverse Fashion Items
item.description: Recent advances in image generation and editing have opened new opportunities for virtual try-on. However, existing methods still struggle to meet complex real-world demands. We present Tstars-Tryon 1.0, a commercial-scale virtual try-on...

item.title: Dual-View Training for Instruction-Following Information Retrieval
item.description: Instruction-following information retrieval (IF-IR) studies retrieval systems that must not only find documents relevant to a query, but also obey explicit user constraints such as required attributes, exclusions, or output preferences....

item.title: UniMesh: Unifying 3D Mesh Understanding and Generation
item.description: Recent advances in 3D vision have led to specialized models for either 3D understanding (e.g., shape classification, segmentation, reconstruction) or 3D generation (e.g., synthesis, completion, and editing). However, these tasks are ofte...

item.title: RDP LoRA: Geometry-Driven Identification for Parameter-Efficient Adaptation in Large Language Models
item.description: Fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) remains structurally uncertain despite parameter-efficient methods such as Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), as the layer-specific roles of internal representations are poorly understood, leading to heu...

item.title: LoopCTR: Unlocking the Loop Scaling Power for Click-Through Rate Prediction
item.description: Scaling Transformer-based click-through rate (CTR) models by stacking more parameters brings growing computational and storage overhead, creating a widening gap between scaling ambitions and the stringent industrial deployment constraint...

item.title: Accurate and scalable exchange-correlation with deep learning
item.description: Density Functional Theory (DFT) underpins much of modern computational chemistry and materials science. Yet, the reliability of DFT-derived predictions of experimentally measurable properties remains fundamentally limited by the need to...

item.title: Stargazer: A Scalable Model-Fitting Benchmark Environment for AI Agents under Astrophysical Constraints
item.description: The rise of autonomous AI agents suggests that dynamic benchmark environments with built-in feedback on scientifically grounded tasks are needed to evaluate the capabilities of these agents in research work. We introduce Stargazer, a sca...

item.title: Micro Language Models Enable Instant Responses
item.description: Edge devices such as smartwatches and smart glasses cannot continuously run even the smallest 100M-1B parameter language models due to power and compute constraints, yet cloud inference introduces multi-second latencies that break the il...

item.title: SPRITE: From Static Mockups to Engine-Ready Game UI
item.description: Game UI implementation requires translating stylized mockups into interactive engine entities. However, current "Screenshot-to-Code" tools often struggle with the irregular geometries and deep visual hierarchies typical of game interface...

item.title: CoInteract: Physically-Consistent Human-Object Interaction Video Synthesis via Spatially-Structured Co-Generation
item.description: Synthesizing human--object interaction (HOI) videos has broad practical value in e-commerce, digital advertising, and virtual marketing. However, current diffusion models, despite their photorealistic rendering capability, still frequent...

item.title: SmartPhotoCrafter: Unified Reasoning, Generation and Optimization for Automatic Photographic Image Editing
item.description: Traditional photographic image editing typically requires users to possess sufficient aesthetic understanding to provide appropriate instructions for adjusting image quality and camera parameters. However, this paradigm relies on explici...

item.title: CityRAG: Stepping Into a City via Spatially-Grounded Video Generation
item.description: We address the problem of generating a 3D-consistent, navigable environment that is spatially grounded: a simulation of a real location. Existing video generative models can produce a plausible sequence that is consistent with a text (T2...

item.title: Code-Switching Information Retrieval: Benchmarks, Analysis, and the Limits of Current Retrievers
item.description: Code-switching is a pervasive linguistic phenomenon in global communication, yet modern information retrieval systems remain predominantly designed for, and evaluated within, monolingual contexts. To bridge this critical disconnect, we p...

item.title: MoVE: Translating Laughter and Tears via Mixture of Vocalization Experts in Speech-to-Speech Translation
item.description: Recent Speech-to-Speech Translation (S2ST) systems achieve strong semantic accuracy yet consistently strip away non-verbal vocalizations (NVs), such as laughter and crying that convey pragmatic intent, which severely limits real-world ut...

item.title: ClawNet: Human-Symbiotic Agent Network for Cross-User Autonomous Cooperation
item.description: Current AI agent frameworks have made remarkable progress in automating individual tasks, yet all existing systems serve a single user. Human productivity rests on the social and organizational relationships through which people coordina...

item.title: TEMPO: Scaling Test-time Training for Large Reasoning Models
item.description: Test-time training (TTT) adapts model parameters on unlabeled test instances during inference time, which continuously extends capabilities beyond the reach of offline training. Despite initial gains, existing TTT methods for LRMs platea...

item.title: Understanding and Enforcing Weight Disentanglement in Task Arithmetic
item.description: Task arithmetic provides an efficient, training-free way to edit pre-trained models, yet lacks a fundamental theoretical explanation for its success. The existing concept of ``weight disentanglement" describes the ideal outcome of non-in...

item.title: Predicting integers from continuous parameters
item.description: We study the problem of predicting numeric labels that are constrained to the integers or to a subrange of the integers. For example, the number of up-votes on social media posts, or the number of bicycles available at a public rental st...
